title: Paltr Carvin
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Paltr_Carvin.jpg]] |name=Paltr Carvin |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 32|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 32]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] **[[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Carvin leads, and will lead for life.''"
"''You say that as if ''life'' is a synonym for ''long''.|Delak Krennel and Ysanne Isard|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 34|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 34]]}} '''Paltr Carvin''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who held the rank of [[General/Legends|General]] in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Tribune]] of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Carvin and the council seized control after Interim [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] was accused of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] and fled [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Working with [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], Carvin put out a warrant for Pestage's arrest, and as a result the former [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] was captured on [[Ciutric IV]]. Carvin appointed [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]] to retrieve Pestage and bring him back to Coruscant, but the ensuing [[evacuation of Ciutric]] prevented that. Pestage had been rescued by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agents, but the agents themselves had been forced to stay on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] due to the presence of the [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]] ''[[Binder]]''. Krennel initially failed to take control of the situation, however, turning it into a fiasco. Meanwhile, Isard had Carvin's closest colleagues [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]], before having the general himself arrested. As control over the Empire was wrested from his grasp, Carvin was taken to the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' [[Prison/Legends|prison]] facility, where he was to be reconditioned to serve Isard loyally. ==Biography== ===Removal of Pestage=== {{Quote|If Pestage is a traitor, then ''we'' are the state.|Paltr Carvin|}} A [[General/Legends|general]] in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], Paltr Carvin was appointed to the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] at some stage around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' After [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] first [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|death]] over the forest [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the council and [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] became embroiled in a brief power struggle for control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with Pestage eventually taking the throne for himself as Interim [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. The council, eager to overthrow Pestage and take the reins of the Empire themselves, became informally allied with [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], who acted as a neutral intermediary between it and Pestage. Following the disastrous [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Brentaal IV]], the council, headed by Carvin, was summoned to a meeting by Isard. She insinuated that Pestage had entered into a pact with the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and intended to effectively turn the Empire over to them. Specifically, Isard suspected that Pestage had met with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the New Republic on the crime-ridden [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Axxila/Legends|Axxila]] and had subsequently given orders to let her escape.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 31|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 31]] Carvin was dubious, and when Isard informed him that she had obtained incriminating data files from "confidential sources" he accused her of being a liar. Isard did not balk at the accusation, and instead insisted that her prime interest was state security. Musing over her report, Carvin surmised that the council would have to rule in Pestage's stead, if what Isard said was true. Council member [[Challer]] was quick to caution the general, however, and went on to question Isard. The director requested that the council strip Emperor Pestage of power and put an end to his treason, and Carvin complied with her request. [[File:Tribunemeeting.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Carvin and the tribunal.]] As a result, Isard was given ''carte blanche'' to ground [[Starship/Legends|ships]] in order to prevent Pestage's escape from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Meanwhile, Carvin and his tribunal charged Pestage with treason, put out a warrant for his arrest, and assumed control of the Galactic Empire. Since Carvin headed the tribunal, which effectively took control of the Empire. In his new position as [[Tribune]], Carvin sported the [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia]] of an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]].''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' Despite the curfew, Pestage was able to escape to [[Ciutric IV]], but was subsequently placed in custody by [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Brothic]]. Isard prepared to send her own men to "recover" him, to apparently stand trial on Coruscant. Carvin would have none of it, however, and ordered Isard to have her people merely secure Pestage and not transport him. At the same time, he had [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]] travel to Ciutric IV in the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Reckoning (Imperial II-class)|''Reckoning'']], to fetch Pestage personally.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]] Isard protested at first, and then requested permission to personally observe Pestage's retrieval. Carvin was incredulous, questioning Isard as to whether she actually had any responsibilities to attend to on Coruscant itself. He and his colleagues denied Isard permission, ordering her to leave the task to Krennel and attend to her duties on the capital. The director disobeyed the tribunal, however, and sent her agents to Ciutric IV. Simultaneously, [[Commando Team One|Republic commandos]] attacked the planet and broke Pestage out of custody. Both Krennel and Isard's men had failed to stop the enemy, but the participation of the Intel Director's [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]], ''[[Binder]]''—which was able to prevent ships from entering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]—had kept the Republic from escaping and had forced them to retreat to the wilderness of Ciutric IV. ===Arrest and imprisonment=== {{Quote|I will ''never'' become your plaything.''"
"''Oh, General Carvin, I think you will find you will become all manner of things when you reach the ''Lusankya'' facility.|Paltr Carvin and Ysanne Isard|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]]}} [[File:Isards_plaything.jpg|thumb|right|Paltr Carvin and Ysanne Isard.]] Carvin and the tribunal placed sole blame for the fiasco on Isard's shoulders and fiercely upbraided her for disobedience. The general warned the director that if she did not cooperate with the tribunal in the future, her career under their rule would be stopped short and abruptly terminated. Carvin then provided Krennel with more [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] with which to smoke the Republic agents out, but the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] craft were subsequently destroyed by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. Because of the tribunal's criticism of her actions and with her position as Intelligence Director in jeopardy, Isard decided to accelerate her timetable for the elimination of the council. While Krennel was engaging Rogue Squadron over Ciutric, Isard was eliminating the tribunes, one by one. She had her agents [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Tribunes Challer and [[Plumba]], alarming Carvin. Yet before he could act, Isard sent [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guards]] to apprehend him. The general was beaten by the guards before being brought before the triumphant Isard. He could do little more than cry treason, helpless before the director. Isard planned to make Carvin serve her as she stepped into his role as ruler of the Empire, but the general insisted that he would not cooperate. The director was not fazed at all, as she was confident that a stint in the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' [[Prison/Legends|prison]] facility would make the general change his mind. With that, Carvin was hauled out of Isard's presence and taken into captivity. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|How is it you have so little to do here on Imperial Center?|Paltr Carvin|}} A power-hungry man, Paltr Carvin was eager and quick to take Sate Pestage's role when the Grand Vizier was accused of being a traitor to the Empire. He was, on occasion, pulled back into line by his colleagues, such as Challer, but was essentially considered to be the head of the Empire following Pestage's departure from Coruscant. His leadership initially inspired confidence in others, such as Delak Krennel, but he would later shift blame to cover his own mistakes, shaking the faith some people had in him. Carvin thought Ysanne Isard insignificant and was quick to deflate her arguments during meetings. The feeling was mutual on Isard's end—the Director of Imperial Intelligence considered the general a fool. Carvin was quick to shoot down Isard's request to travel to Ciutric IV and, together with his colleagues, mocked the director by reeling off a list of objectives she had failed to accomplish. Later, when beaten and [[Blood/Legends|bloodied]] before Isard, he remained defiant and refused to serve her, although she was confident that the ''Lusankya'' prison facility would help change his mind. ==Behind the scenes== Paltr Carvin made his first appearance in [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 32|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 32]] written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and penciled by [[Steve Crespo]], the first issue of [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement|''Mandatory Retirement'']], the final story arc of the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. Carvin made appearances in the three subsequent issues of ''Mandatory Retirement'', all of which were likewise written by Stackpole with pencils by Crespo and [[John Nadeau]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Paltr Carvin}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 32|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 32]] {{1st}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 34|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 34]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Paltr Carvin}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' {{1stID|Paltr}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Paltr Carvin }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Carvin, Paltr}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Army generals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Imperial Ruling Council members]] [[Category:Males]]
